title: PR annotations
problem: CI/platform maintainers expect scanner results to appear where code review happens; GitHub Issues export alone creates a separate backlog instead of actionable PR feedback.
behaviorChange: Add SARIF/GitHub Checks/PR annotation output so measured findings can appear inline or as check-run summaries, while keeping local artifacts as the source of truth.

title: Baselines and waivers
problem: CI/platform maintainers need a way to tolerate known debt without hiding new regressions; without explicit baselines, teams will disable noisy gates.
behaviorChange: Add structured baseline, ignore, and waiver records with finding IDs, reason, owner, expiry, and audit visibility; gates fail on new or expired findings, not accepted debt.

title: Task-flow capture
problem: AI agents and builders will audit real apps whose important UI states only appear after login, clicks, form input, or navigation; route-level capture misses menus, modals, validation, and post-action states.
behaviorChange: Accept named task-flow recipes or recorded browser steps, capture each reachable state, and report unreachable or unaudited steps explicitly.

title: Sensitive data redaction
problem: Builders and teams will hesitate to run audits on real apps if screenshots, DOM, source snippets, or exported issues may expose PII, secrets, customer data, or proprietary content.
behaviorChange: Add configurable redaction for captures and exports, with clear markers showing redacted evidence and an option to retain full local evidence only.

title: Human verdict loop
problem: Non-designer builders and CI maintainers need a durable way to accept, reject, correct, or defer judged findings; otherwise surface cannot reliably learn from gated decisions or reduce repeated false positives.
behaviorChange: Add structured adjudication commands and artifact fields for verdict, rationale, owner, and reuse policy, feeding self-grounding metrics and future prioritization.
